headline: Chip Stocks Ignite Tuesday Rally as Semis Surge More Than 6%
body_html:
Semiconductors dominated Tuesday's session with a vengeance, lifting the broader market as the sector posted a gain of more than six percent — by far the strongest performance across any segment of the market. Memory names led the charge: Sandisk surged nearly 14.3% and Micron climbed 12.2%, while semiconductor-equipment maker Ichor Holdings and fabless chipmaker MaxLinear each added roughly 14.5% and 14.7%, respectively. The move appeared tied to renewed appetite for technology hardware following a stretch of weakness, with Wall Street's attention sharpening ahead of upcoming earnings reports.

The rotation was unmistakable. Energy notched a solid 2.0% gain — partly lifted by geopolitical tension in the Red Sea, where Houthi warnings forced Saudi crude tankers to reverse course — while Materials and Industrials added modestly. The defensive tilt reversed, however, as Software shed 1.7% and Consumer retreated 0.75%. Homebuilders dipped fractionally. Healthcare was essentially flat, masking pockets of sharp selling within the space.

On the decliner side, MSCI led all losers with a 10.4% drop, an outsized move for a financial-data firm that stood in stark contrast to the session's risk-on tenor. Market breadth was positive but not emphatic — 266 advancers against 231 decliners in a 499-name universe — a reminder that Tuesday's rally was largely a semiconductor story rather than a broad-based advance.

briefs.headline: Houthi Escalation Forces Saudi Crude Tankers Off Course
briefs.body: At least two tankers carrying Saudi crude reversed direction in the Red Sea after receiving direct warnings from Houthi forces, Reuters reported. The militant group emailed shipping companies instructing them to avoid Saudi ports, marking a fresh front in the broader US-Iran conflict. Asian refiners are now exploring Suez Canal routing to secure Saudi oil supplies.

briefs.headline: Goldman Sachs Opens Private-Markets Access to Wealthy Clients
briefs.body: Goldman Sachs launched a dedicated private-markets platform aimed at high-net-worth investors seeking exposure to pre-IPO companies. The initiative reflects surging demand among wealthy individuals to participate in the kind of growth stories that firms such as SpaceX and Stripe have represented before any public listing.

briefs.headline: SpaceX Names Earnings Date, Triggering Major Share Lock-Up Expiry
briefs.body: SpaceX announced its first formal earnings date, an event that simultaneously triggers the initial lock-up expiration for millions of shares held by early investors and employees. The disclosure adds a new layer of near-term supply uncertainty for one of the most closely watched private companies in the world.

briefs.headline: Bloom Energy Stands Out as Tuesday's Single Biggest Percentage Gainer
briefs.body: Bloom Energy topped the leaderboard with a nearly 15% advance, outpacing even the surging semiconductor names. The fuel-cell manufacturer's move coincided with heightened energy-security headlines tied to Red Sea shipping disruptions, though no company-specific catalyst was immediately identified as the driver.

briefs.headline: Iran's Supreme Leader Situation Draws Closer International Scrutiny
briefs.body: Iran's president confirmed that interaction with the country's injured Supreme Leader is increasing daily, Reuters reported. The disclosure comes as Iran separately warned Bulgaria against facilitating US military operations in the region following reports of American aircraft being stationed there, adding to already elevated geopolitical tensions.

briefs.headline: Cramer Reaffirms Eli Lilly Conviction After GLP-1 Legal Challenge
briefs.body: Television personality Jim Cramer publicly maintained his support for Eli Lilly following a lawsuit filed by a rival targeting the company's GLP-1 drug franchise. Cramer argued the legal action does not materially alter the investment case, drawing attention to the competitive dynamics continuing to unfold in the obesity and diabetes drug market.
